title: ✈️ Bidding in plane sight
body_text: The invisible hand’s using a five-finger discount… Shoplifting has grown more common in the US according to a new survey from loan comparison site LendingTree, as the share of Americans who say they’ve shoplifted rises to nearly a third — up from just 23% who said the same in 2024. The increase was particularly pronounced among the youngest generations, while some 90% of respondents who said they’ve shoplifted blamed inflation and the current economy. Markets were relatively flat, continuing the relatively muted week, extending a relatively boring month. We here at Snacks hope everyone on Wall Street is enjoying their vacation! As we head into the penultimate day of the Farnborough Air Show — a biennial event where aerospace and defense industry titans, innovators, and people who just like planes travel from across the globe to marvel at new technological developments and, more importantly, sign huge deals — it won’t just be the show organizers who hope they can stick the landing. This year’s attendance is expected to outstrip the record 100,000-strong turnout from 2024, but few among the throng will have as much riding on the event as execs from Boeing and Airbus , the world’s leading airplane manufacturers, which rely on Farnborough to boost their order book each year: According to Reuters , US manufacturer Boeing announced 2,057 aircraft orders at the British expo from 2010 to 2024, while its European rival Airbus secured 1,984 across the same time frame. On the first day of this year’s show alone, the companies secured a combined total of 234 aircraft orders worth some $48.8 billion, per figures from UK industry trade group ADS. Still, as anyone in the industry will tell you (at least when they get back from Farnborough), there’s a big difference between securing hundreds of aircraft orders and managing to actually make those deliveries. Despite both manufacturers continuing to rack up orders of late, a combination of geopolitical instability, trade restrictions, increased demand, constrained supply of key materials, and numerous other factors has made fulfilling aircraft commitments trickier, with annual deliveries yet to reach their pre-pandemic levels again. As Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg succinctly put it in an interview with the Wall Street Journal ahead of this week: “Orders are not our challenge, our challenge is getting these orders delivered.” Although the Farnborough Air Show might be good for securing business, schmoozing, and making splashy announcements, there remains an underlying production bottleneck issue in the aviation industry compared to previous years, with the International Air Transport Association highlighting a delivery shortfall of at least 5,300 aircraft and a record 17,000-jet backlog in its global outlook report at the end of last year. 🥊 Kelly: Prediction markets * put English boxer Josh Kelly as the 79% favorite against Ireland’s Caoimhín Agyarko ahead of their fight on Anthony Joshua’s undercard this weekend — a bout that Kelly promised will be “spiteful” and a “ spectacle .” *Event contracts are offered through Robinhood Derivatives, LLC — probabilities referenced or sourced from KalshiEx LLC or ForecastEx LLC. That’s the share of songs uploaded to Deezer on peak days that are generated by AI, the music streamer just announced . Meanwhile, Netflix revealed that around 300 of its titles in 2026 so far have incorporated some form of generative AI, mostly in post-production, as part of its Q2 report last week . AMD and Anthropic signed a bumper deal in which the former agreed to invest $5 billion in the AI giant, while the Claude company will buy tens of billions of dollars’ worth of AMD’s servers, per the WSJ . Spain’s 1-0 slog against Argentina was the most-watched soccer telecast in US history (38.9M on Fox) and Spanish-language TV history (23.9M via Telemundo and Peacock). It was also one of the worst ads for “the beautiful game” across the whole tournament. Marlboro and Parliament maker Philip Morris cut its 2026 outlook, doubling down on its Zyn products as the nicotine pouch landscape heats up . Google Q2 revenue and earnings beat Wall Street expectations; cloud unit revenue came in ahead of estimates, market seems to hate it . Tesla reported EPS of $0.31, lower than expectations of $0.51; revenue comes in at $28.24 billion , higher than Wall Street's estimate of $25.71 billion ~80,000 people saw “The Odyssey” across its opening two days in Greece — a post-pandemic record per the film’s distributor… and around the same number who filled Athens’ Panathinaiko Stadium during the opening ceremony of the first modern Olympics in 1896. Earnings from RTX , Honeywell , American Airlines , Lockheed Martin , PG&E Corp , Cleveland - Cliffs , Freeport McMoRan , and Comcast are all due in the busy premarket. Intel and Digital Realty slated to release quarterly results after the close.

title: 🏋️‍♂️ Weight-Loss Wars
body_text: Remember when the fanciest thing you could add to your car was heated seats? Wealthy Americans are increasingly turning everyday SUVs into armored vehicles. Outfitting your car into something from a James Bond movie — or at least something that can protect you from a small handgun — starts at $50,000 and can easily top six figures. But some customers aren’t stopping there and are armoring multiple vehicles. Stocks rose on Tuesday, with the S&P 500 gaining just under 1% and the Nasdaq 100 up almost 2%. Novo Nordisk — the pharmaceutical giant behind Ozempic and Wegovy — filed a lawsuit against its top rival, Eli Lilly , on Tuesday, accusing Lilly of using outdated trial data to claim that its drugs are superior. Lilly’s weight loss shot, Zepbound, did in fact beat Novo’s Wegovy in a head-to-head trial last year. But Novo has since gotten approval for a higher dose that has been shown to shed more pounds and has not yet been directly compared to Lilly’s competing product. According to Novo, it asked Lilly to take down the ads in April, more than a month after its higher dose shot got approved in the US. Lilly did not respond to those requests, Novo said. “As new and more effective treatment options become available, people deserve accurate information that reflects the latest scientific evidence and helps them make informed care decisions," Novo’s top lawyer, John F. Kuckelman said in a statement. It’s not the first time Novo has used the courts to win back market share: Earlier this year, the company sued Hims & Hers for selling a dupe of its Wegovy pill . Hims eventually took down the pill from its offerings and partnered with Novo to distribute the pharma giant’s branded products. Denmark-based Novo, which was the first to bring a GLP-1 to market, has been outsold by its American rival Lilly for over a year now. That’s evident in their market performance — Novo is down more than 23% in the past year while Lilly swelled by over 50%, reaching a market capitalization of over $1 trillion. In the first quarter of 2026, sales of Lilly’s GLP-1 shots hit $12.9 billion compared to Novo’s $7.3 billion . Analysts estimate that gap widened in the second quarter. The lawsuit “does not seem like a decision that comes from a place of strength,” said Brian Mulberry, chief market strategist at Zacks Investment Management, which holds Lilly. “NVO is clearly worried.” Novo does have one glimmer of hope: its Wegovy pill appears to be outselling Lilly’s weight loss pill, Foundayo . 🏀Lebron James’ next team: What team will LeBron play for in his 24th NBA season? The market gives the Cleveland Cavaliers a 38% * chance of getting the basketball legend on their roster, while Miami Heat are at a 36% chance. *Event contracts are offered through Robinhood Derivatives, LLC — probabilities referenced or sourced from KalshiEx LLC or ForecastEx LLC. Quarterly sales of “Magic: The Gathering” eclipsed $500 million for the first time in its 30-plus year history, Hasbro reported Tuesday . The US is on the verge of losing its place as the world’s leading “farm superpower,” the FT reports . In 2025, Brazil exported just $2 billion less agricultural products — including fruits, vegetables and grains — than the US did, compared to a $56 billion gap in 2021. Kraft Heinz and Disney have struck a deal, the WSJ reports . The food giant's products will be sold at Disney’s theme parks in North America in exchange for the ability to use Disney’s characters on some Kraft Heinz goods in stores. General Motors reported results that beat Wall Street estimates on Tuesday and raised its outlook, driven by higher sales of its most profitable models and fewer sales of its money-losing electric vehicles. South Korea's exports ​for the ‌first 20 days of July ​jumped 52.3% ​from a year ⁠earlier, led by a surge in shipments of semiconductors. Philip Morris International and GE Vernova slated to release quarterly results ahead of the open. IBM , CSX , Texas Instruments , Tesla , Google , Crown Castle , ServiceNow , and Southwest Airlines slated to release quarterly results after the close.

title: ⚔️ Sing In Me, O Muse, Of AMC
body_text: There’s not much stopping AI companies from scraping any shard of text you put on the internet and using it to train their models. No, not even writing in wingdings . Ghost Font tries to solve that problem by rendering text as a video of clusters of black dots that scroll up on a background of dots that scroll down, Popular Science reports . Whether the ensuing headache is worth it is up to you. AMC Entertainment stock closed 26.29 % higher on Monday after the company reported its highest-grossing quarter in its 106-year history, handily beating Wall Street estimates. Here’s the sometimes meme stock’s Q2 by the numbers: Revenue of $1.59 billion (compared to analyst estimates of $1.47 billion.) Adjusted EPS of $0.14 (estimate of -$0.03.) Those strong operational results were driven by, pretty simply, more people going to the movies: AMC said attendance was up nearly 14% in the quarter and EBITDA surged to $321.4 million, up nearly 70% year-to-date. On the earnings call on Monday, AMC Theatres CEO Adam Aron said “Spider-Man: Brand New Day,” “Dune: Part Three,” and the upcoming Avengers movie can continue the positive momentum. “We believe that movie theaters will enjoy the full 12 months of 2026, their strongest yet post-pandemic year at both the domestic box office and at the global box office.” It can’t but help AMC that its earnings results come the Monday after Christopher Nolan’s “The Odyssey” had the biggest domestic opening weekend of the year for a live action film, crashing the AMC app and leaving fans struggling for tickets, especially for IMAX showings. It earned $124.5 million in North America and an estimated $264.1 million globally. Across the industry, domestic movie ticket sales are up about 10 percent year-on-year, according to Variety . It seems that the trend pieces about how people are going back to the movies again are grounded in reality. And to see “The Odyssey,” people aren’t just going to movie theaters – they’re going in massive numbers to a very small number of IMAX theaters. Nolan’s movie was filmed on IMAX and on Monday’s earnings call AMC CEO Adam Aron noted that while these larger-format screens represent only about 8% of AMC’s total, IMAX showings of the Odyssey, this weekend, generated more than 50% of our total ticket growth for the film. Aron said AMC is looking to increase the number of extra large screens in 100 to 250 auditoriums over the next several years using third party capital. That is all very good news for the movie industry generally and AMC specifically. The company’s shares are up nearly 50% over the past six months on the back of the relatively good news about their underlying business of selling tickets and snacks to people who want to watch movies. But zoom out and things look less rosy: the stock is down 30% over the last 12 months and has dropped more than 98% from its 2021 meme stock peak. Much-better-than-solid operational results, it turns out, can be good for a stock, but nothing will compare to frenzied forum buying. 🎬 Avengers: Doomsday: Usually when you release a trailer, you generally want people to leave that three to five minute teaser considerably more excited about the quality of the film you intend to distribute. Yesterday’s release of the “Avengers: Doomsday” trailer did not exactly sell traders on the film’s prospects; the probability the movie finishes with a Rotten Tomatoes score above 85 dropped from 49% pre-trailer to 42% right after .* 📺 Paramount: Markets were relatively unfazed yesterday after a judge ordered Paramount to pause its acquisition of Warner Bros. while the court hears a round of anti-trust arguments from a group of states. The probability that Paramount seals the deal was flat at 82% , indicating that a judge at least hearing out the suit was largely priced in. *Event contracts are offered through Robinhood Derivatives, LLC — probabilities referenced or sourced from KalshiEx LLC or ForecastEx LLC. A leather jacket worn by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang sold for $960,000 . Sotheby’s said the 65 bids they received for the jacket helped push the sale price well over the pre-sale estimate of between $40,000 and $60,000. Oil prices dropped after Iran said mediators have been in touch to find ways to de-escalate hostilities with the US following more than a week of US strikes in response to Iranian attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. Morgan Stanley is cashing in on the AI-debt boom, becoming the go-to Wall Street bank for AI-industry players like Broadcom , Meta , and TeraWulf . After wrapping up the most-watched World Cup ever, FIFA is preparing to open the bidding war for the US rights to televise the 2030 and 2034 tournaments . King Felipe VI of Spain is the tallest reigning monarch, standing 6′ 5.5′′. D.R.
